title: Typedef support (with wrong syntax)
body:
This work is based on #1389. In order to decouple the GLR discussion from the rest of the original PR, this adds typedef support with a wrong syntax, that doesn't require a GLR parser, so we can merge the rest of the PR and figure out what to do about GLR later.

The following would re-enable the correct syntax, using GLR:

```
--- frontends/verilog/verilog_parser.y
+++ frontends/verilog/verilog_parser.y
@@ -180,7 +180,9 @@ struct specify_rise_fall {
 %right UNARY_OPS
 
 %define parse.error verbose
-%define parse.lac full
+//%define parse.lac full
+%glr-parser
+%expect 21
 
 %nonassoc FAKE_THEN
 %nonassoc TOK_ELSE
@@ -292,7 +294,7 @@ hierarchical_id:
        };
 
 hierarchical_type_id:
-       '(' hierarchical_id ')' { $$ = $2; };
+       hierarchical_id { $$ = $1; };
 
 module:
        attr TOK_MODULE TOK_ID {
```

Compared to #1389 this version doesn't have the two rr-conflicts.
